sal25_C01_donanemab_aducanumab_head_to_h.query: donanemab aducanumab head-to-head amyloid clearance trial
sal25_C01_donanemab_aducanumab_head_to_h.note: 39998021 = Khartabil & Awaness 2025-02-08 review 'Targeting Amyloid Pathology in Early Alzheimer's: The Promise of Donanemab-Azbt' (DOI 10.3390/pharmacy13010023), genuinely PRIOR to sal25's own publication (PMID 40390253, May 2025, DOI 10.1002/alz.70293). Its abstract explicitly states: '(4) TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 4: A head-to-head comparison with aducanumab revealed superior amyloid plaque clearance with donanemab.' -- i.e. the topline RESULT sal25's C01/C03 report as part of a 'first-ever head-to-head' novelty claim was ALREADY public (via conference/press disclosure) before sal25's peer-reviewed paper. The STUDY DESIGN priority (first head-to-head RCT) is not refuted, but the RESULT's novelty at time of this peer-reviewed publication is weaker than the sem_findings 'novel' tag implies -- a real, literature-grounded nuance a compiler-fidelity check could not catch.

the25_C01_ptau_meta_analysis_diagnostic.query: plasma phosphorylated tau meta-analysis diagnostic accuracy Alzheimer disease
the25_C01_ptau_meta_analysis_diagnostic.note: IMPORTANT METHODOLOGY FINDING: all 5 returned articles are dated FEBRUARY-JUNE 2026 (per get_article_metadata), i.e. AFTER the25's own 2025 publication (DOI 10.1016/S1474-4422(25)00227-3) -- despite the query's date_to=2024/12/31 filter. The PubMed search_articles tool's date_to parameter did NOT reliably exclude post-cutoff results in this environment (it appears to filter on a different date field than publication_date, or not at all for some records). CONSEQUENCE: get_article_metadata's own publication_date must be checked per-candidate before treating any hit as 'prior literature' -- never trust date_to alone. None of these 5 candidates qualify as prior art for the25 (they are subsequent, contemporaneous meta-analyses on the same topic -- showing the field became crowded shortly after the25, not that the25 over-claimed at time of writing). the25's novelty claim is NOT falsified by this search; correctly reported as no-prior-evidence-found, not as a caught over-claim.

tit26_C07_elecsys_lumipulse_passing_babl.query: Elecsys Lumipulse p-tau217 comparison Passing-Bablok Alzheimer

sal26_C01_emtbr_tau243_staging.note: 42189519 = sal26's OWN paper ('Plasma eMTBR-tau243 and %p-tau217 for Biological Staging of Alzheimer Disease', DOI 10.1001/jamaneurol.2026.1405 -- matches sal26 sources.yaml exactly) -- SELF-MATCH, excluded. 41170658 ('Combining CSF MTBR-tau243 and plasma pTau217 ratio enhances the prediction of continuous regional tau PET burden', Nov 2025) and 40164726 ('Plasma MTBR-tau243 biomarker identifies tau tangle pathology in Alzheimer's disease', Mar 2025) are GENUINELY PRIOR (both predate sal26's 2026 paper) and establish that the MTBR-tau243 biomarker's tau-PET/tangle-pathology-predicting utility was already published before sal26. CONCLUSION: sal26's 'first reported concordance between a combined model and PET-based staging' claim is an INCREMENTAL extension (a new staging-scheme application) of an already-established biomarker, not a fully de-novo finding -- partial novelty, not the strong 'first' framing alone would suggest.

ahm26b_C02_ad_mortality_cdc_wonder.query: Alzheimer disease age-adjusted mortality rate United States CDC WONDER trend
ahm26b_C02_ad_mortality_cdc_wonder.note: 24 total hits show this is a topically crowded space (many groups publish CDC WONDER AD-mortality-trend analyses); titles/dates of the 5 shown were not individually verified against ahm26b's exact 1999-2020/22-year window (DOI 10.1097/WAD.0000000000000712) due to time budget -- reported as 'topically non-novel at the broad-method level, specific-window duplication unconfirmed', not as a resolved over-claim catch. Flagged pending for a deeper pass.

ahm26b_C01_ad_mortality_trend_general.query: Alzheimer disease age-adjusted mortality rate trend United States 1999 2020
ahm26b_C01_ad_mortality_trend_general.note: Cycle-3 C5 addition: ahm26b C01 was sem-flagged 'novel' (12-pending backlog) but never checked. Real get_article_metadata dates show this is a topically crowded space: e.g. PMID 39178013 (2024-08-13, 'Divergent Mortality Patterns Associated With Dementia in the United States: 1999-...') genuinely PREDATES ahm26b's own confirmed pub date (2026-02-24, PMID 41773884, DOI 10.1097/WAD.0000000000000712 -- fetched live via PubMed, not the slug's '26'). ahm26b's own novelty note narrowly scopes the claim to 'first tabulation of this specific AAPC'; broadly, AD mortality trend analysis over a comparable window is well-established prior literature.

ahm26b_C03_ad_mortality_sex_disparity.query: Alzheimer disease mortality sex disparity female male United States
ahm26b_C03_ad_mortality_sex_disparity.note: PMID 25946701 ('Trends in Disparity by Sex and Race/Ethnicity for the Leading Causes of Death in the United States-1999-2010', 2016, DOI 10.1097/PHH.0000000000000267) directly reports sex-disparity trends for Alzheimer disease mortality using the same NVSS/CDC lineage, 10 years prior to ahm26b's confirmed 2026-02-24 pub date. Genuine on-topic precedent.

ahm26b_C04_ad_mortality_race_disparity.note: Same PMID 25946701 (2016) directly reports race/ethnicity AD-mortality disparity trends, prior to ahm26b's confirmed 2026-02-24 pub date. Genuine on-topic precedent for the race/ethnicity breakdown.

ahm26b_C05_ad_mortality_age_85plus.query: Alzheimer disease mortality older adults age 85 crude rate United States
ahm26b_C05_ad_mortality_age_85plus.note: PMID 22860569 ('Burden of Alzheimer's disease-related mortality in the United States, 1999-2008', 2012, DOI 10.1111/j.1532-5415.2012.04056.x) is a near-identical prior CDC-mortality-burden study, 14 years before ahm26b's confirmed 2026-02-24 pub date, of the same general age-stratified design.

ahm26b_C06_ad_mortality_rural_urban.note: Two direct precedents predate ahm26b (confirmed pub date 2026-02-24): PMID 33368161 ('Rural-Urban Disparities in Mortality from Alzheimer's and Related Dementias in the United States', 2020-12-24, DOI 10.1111/jgs.16996) and PMID 35466938 ('Alzheimer's Disease Mortality as a Function of Urbanization Level: 1999-2019', 2022, DOI 10.3233/JAD-215586) -- both directly on-topic, 4-6 years prior.

ahm26b_C07_ad_mortality_census_region.note: PMID 39425740 ('Evaluating the Regional and Demographic Variations in Dementia-Related Mortality', 2024-Oct, DOI 10.1002/gps.70004) is a direct precedent, ~16 months prior to ahm26b's confirmed 2026-02-24 pub date.

ahm26b_C08_ad_mortality_state_level.query: Alzheimer disease mortality state-level variation United States
ahm26b_C08_ad_mortality_state_level.note: PMID 39645376 ('The burden of diseases, injuries, and risk factors by state in the USA, 1990-2021', 2024-12-07, Lancet, DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(24)01446-6) is a direct state-level burden-of-disease precedent (includes AD) predating ahm26b's confirmed 2026-02-24 pub date by ~14 months.

huu25_C05_ancestry_specific_deg_oligo.query: APOE ancestry-specific differential gene expression oligodendrocyte Alzheimer
huu25_C05_ancestry_specific_deg_oligo.note: PMID 40937943 ('Ancestral genomic functional differences in oligodendroglia: implications for Alzheimer's disease', Alzheimer's & Dementia, 2025-Sep, DOI 10.1002/alz.70593) and its earlier preprint PMID 39678342 (Research Square, 2024-12-04, DOI 10.21203/rs.3.rs-5338140/v1) both PREDATE huu25's own confirmed pub date (2025-11-20, PMID 41332786). Same broad topic (ancestry-specific genomic/oligodendroglia findings in AD) as huu25 C05's 'first molecular-level ancestry-stratified DGE result' novelty claim. huu25's own sem novelty note narrows the claim to 'this ERC dataset specifically', which this search cannot rule out or confirm (different cohort) -- reported as prior_literature_found on the broad topic, with that dataset-specific hedge preserved verbatim.
